risky_phi_b_tail_reconsideration.summary: Replay, snapshot, and isolation doctrine now provide explicit continuity and containment law, but they do not clear the risky Φ-B tail by themselves.

upsilon_a_start.summary: Υ-A can now begin on top of explicit replay, snapshot, and isolation doctrine rather than inventing continuity assumptions ad hoc.

zeta_blocker_reduction.summary: The safe trio removes replay, snapshot, and isolation ambiguity, but Ζ still depends on multi-version, hotswap, distributed authority, operator transaction, downgrade, provenance, and trust maturity.

risky_phi_b_tail_readiness.title: MULTI_VERSION_COEXISTENCE_REVIEW-0
risky_phi_b_tail_readiness.summary: Safe Φ-B is necessary but not sufficient; coexistence still waits on versioning, release contract, pipeline/archive continuity, and downgrade law.

risky_phi_b_tail_readiness.title: HOTSWAP_BOUNDARY_REVIEW-0
risky_phi_b_tail_readiness.summary: Hotswap still risks lifecycle, state, and operator-authority shortcuts even as a review prompt.

risky_phi_b_tail_readiness.title: DISTRIBUTED_AUTHORITY_FOUNDATIONS_REVIEW-0
risky_phi_b_tail_readiness.summary: Distributed authority remains downstream of coexistence, hotswap, operator transaction, downgrade, and publication/trust review.

upsilon_a_readiness.summary: Existing build graph and preset substrate should be frozen constitutionally rather than reinvented.
Current preset and toolchain surfaces already exist and need authority clarification more than replacement.
Release identity and version substrate already exists in canonical docs and release policy surfaces.
Release identity, index, and resolver surfaces already exist and should be aligned into one law-governed band.
Current release identity and update substrate is strong enough to support an explicit contract profile.
The active post-Σ-B plan combines artifact naming and target naming into one prompt; older split references are numbering drift.
The repo already has changelog substrate and now needs explicit governed meaning.
Existing archive, manifest, and pipeline substrate is real and should be formalized without granting publication authority.
These prompts sit just beyond the narrow Υ-A list, but the risky Φ-B tail still depends on them.

zeta_blockers_or_prerequisites: preserved replay/snapshot/isolation caution in later operational work
ownership_projection_cautions: field/ is not equivalent to canonical fields/
schemas/ is not equivalent to canonical schema/
data/packs/ is not equivalent to canonical packs/
canonical versus projected/generated artifacts remains binding
the thin runtime/ root is not automatically canonical
release/control-plane convenience does not create canon or permission
stale numbering or title drift does not override current checkpoint law
the active post-sigma-b checkpoint and next-two-series plan outrank stale upsilon numbering in final_prompt_inventory.json

final_verdict.summary: The repo should continue into Υ-A and its immediate continuity follow-on before reconsidering the risky Φ-B tail.
